question: Is the image formatted as a vertical cross-section infographic?
Does the diagram clearly span from Earth's surface at the bottom to near-Earth orbit at the top?
Are the atmospheric layers clearly labeled with text?
Are the labels restricted to layer names only, avoiding lengthy explanatory annotations or paragraphs?
Are cumulonimbus clouds with thunderstorm features (e.g., lightning, anvil shape) depicted?
Is a commercial airliner depicted in the image?
Is a meteorological sounding balloon depicted?
Is there a visual indicator or label representing the ozone layer?
Is meteor ablation (shooting stars/burning meteors) depicted?
Are auroras (northern/southern lights) depicted?
Is a near-Earth orbit space station or satellite depicted?
Are the cumulonimbus clouds positioned in the lowest atmospheric layer (Troposphere)?
Is the meteorological sounding balloon positioned higher than the commercial airliner (consistent with stratospheric flight)?
Is the ozone layer indicator positioned in the Stratosphere region (above the airliner but below the meteors)?
Is the meteor ablation positioned in the upper atmosphere (Mesosphere), clearly above the ozone layer?
Are the auroras positioned in the high atmosphere (Thermosphere), above the meteor activity?
Is the space station or satellite positioned at the very top of the diagram (Orbit)?
Are the atmospheric layer labels ordered correctly from bottom to top (Troposphere -> Stratosphere -> Mesosphere -> Thermosphere)?
Is the text within the infographic legible and free from severe gibberish?
Are all depicted objects complete and free from severe geometric distortion?
